indicator_text: Replace the current system of hugely diverging bandwidths with a requirement that consultancies and law firms declare clients' expenditure in bandwidths of €10,000.
source_quote: Instead of the current system of hugely diverging bandwidths, consultancies and law firms should have to declare clients' expenditure in bandwidths of €10,000. (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:14-16)
notes: EU-specific currency (€10,000); but the principle (narrower fixed bandwidths for consultancy client expenditure) generalizes.

indicator_text: All registrants (including industry lobby groups, thinktanks and NGOs) should name all government agencies, grant-making foundations, companies and others that contribute to their budget.
source_quote: All registrants (including industry lobby groups, thinktanks and NGOs) should be asked to name all government agencies, grant-making foundations, companies and others that contribute to their budget (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:18-21)
notes: Applies to all registrant types, not just consultancies.

indicator_text: Registrants should specify the amount received from each funding contributor.
source_quote: specify the amount they receive from each of these (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:20-21)
notes: Per-source amount, not just aggregate.

indicator_text: Small private donations from individuals (up to a certain threshold) should be exempt from the funding-source disclosure requirement.
source_quote: Small private donations from individuals (up to a certain threshold) should be exempt. (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:21-22)
notes: Threshold left unspecified by ALTER-EU.

indicator_text: Registrants should be required to list the names of all their lobbyists in the register, not simply those with parliamentary access badges.
section_or_category: Names of lobbyists and revolving door listings
source_quote: Registrants should be required to list the names of all their lobbyists (not simply those with parliamentary access badges) in the register (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:24-26)
notes: Parliament-pass scope is EU-specific; the underlying principle (full lobbyist roster) generalizes.

indicator_text: Registrants should disclose any former public offices that their lobbyists have held, including at the national level.
section_or_category: Names of lobbyists and revolving door listings
source_quote: as well as any former public offices that these lobbyists have held (including at the national level). This is to enable public scrutiny of the revolving door (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:26-28)
notes: Revolving-door disclosure; explicitly includes national-level past offices (not just EU-level).

indicator_text: Organisations should be required to provide precise information on the main legislative proposals they are lobbying on, including a list of official legislative references.
source_quote: Organisations should be required to provide precise information on the main legislative proposals they are lobbying on, including a list of official legislative references. (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:31-33)
notes: Legislative references generalize to bill numbers / docket IDs at state level.

indicator_text: Leaving the issues-lobbied field blank or providing a very general description should not be allowed.
source_quote: Leaving this field blank or providing a very general description, as is currently often the case, should not be allowed. (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:33-34)
notes: Anti-vagueness rule for issue-disclosure fields.

indicator_text: Introduce a more user-friendly disclosure model for issues lobbied on, for example a drop-down list of legislative dossiers.
source_quote: a more user-friendly model for disclosing issues lobbied on should be introduced, for example, a drop-down list of legislative dossiers. (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:34-35)
notes: Form-design recommendation; structured pick-list rather than free text.

indicator_text: Consultancies and law firms should be obliged to provide a list of the main legislative proposals on which they have lobbied, for each of their clients.
source_quote: consultancies and law firms should be obliged to provide a list of the main legislative proposals on which they have been lobbying, for each of their clients. (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:36-37)
notes: Per-client legislative-issue disclosure for consultancies/law firms.

indicator_text: The register secretariat should actively check entry updates and ensure financial information is up-to-date.
source_quote: The register secretariat should actively check entry updates and ensure that the financial information is up-to-date. (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:40-42)
notes: Active staff verification, not passive registrant attestation.

indicator_text: As a starting point, lobby expenses should refer to the most recent calendar year.
source_quote: As a starting point, lobby expenses should refer to the most recent calendar year. (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:42)
notes: Defines reporting period; addresses problem of multi-year-old figures.

indicator_text: Require all registrations to be updated twice a year, on fixed and pre-determined dates.
source_quote: instate a requirement for all registrations to be updated twice a year, on fixed and pre-determined dates, in order to improve transparency and compliance. (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:43-44)
notes: Semi-annual update cadence on fixed dates.

indicator_text: Lobby consultancies and law firms should provide an up-to-date list of clients from the previous six-month period twice a year.
source_quote: a specific requirement for lobby consultancies and law firms to twice a year provide an up-to-date list of clients from the previous six month period. (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:45-49)
notes: Semi-annual rolling client list specific to consultancies/law firms.

indicator_text: New clients should be declared within one month after the start of the contract.
source_quote: New clients should also be declared within a one month period after the start of the contract. (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:50)
notes: Event-driven (not just periodic) reporting trigger for new client engagements.

indicator_text: Conduct regular checks on registrations and enforce against incomplete client lists and unrecognisable/ambiguous client-name abbreviations.
source_quote: ALTER-EU suggests a more ambitious approach with regular checks on registrations and enforcement to prevent these problems. (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:53-57)
notes: Targets two failure modes: incomplete lists and abbreviated/ambiguous client names.

indicator_text: Consultancies and law firms that fail to disclose clients should be suspended from the register and have their Parliamentary passes withdrawn.
source_quote: Consultancies and law firms should be suspended from the register and their Parliamentary passes withdrawn if they fail to disclose clients. (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:57-59)
notes: Enforcement penalty. Parliamentary-pass withdrawal is EU-specific; suspension principle generalizes.

indicator_text: Reject any ad-hoc, derogative or exceptional formula for category I (law firms and lobby consultancies) entities claiming a need for client confidentiality; law firms providing lobbying services should be subject to the same transparency requirements as any other actor involved in lobbying.
source_quote: law firms should be subject to the same transparency requirements as any other kind of actor involved in lobbying. (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:61-66)
notes: Pushes back against proposed confidentiality carve-out for law firms.

indicator_text: Registrants should be required to report which lobby consultancies and law firms have assisted them in their lobbying.
section_or_category: Obliging registrants to disclose lobby consultancies and law firms assisting their lobbying
source_quote: the obligation for registrants to report which lobby consultancies and law firms have assisted them in their lobbying (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:71-73)
notes: Designed to capture activity of unregistered consultancies/firms; reciprocal disclosure from the client side.

indicator_text: Registered organisations should disclose the total number of persons involved in lobbying, including their names.
section_or_category: Tackling the problem of under-reporting the number of lobbyists
source_quote: registered organisations should be asked to disclose the total number of persons involved in lobbying, including their names (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:78-79)
notes: Total headcount + names, not a sampled or badge-only subset.

indicator_text: Registrants should disclose a calculation of total full-time equivalents (FTEs) for the organisation, given that some employees may be engaged in interest representation only part of the time.
section_or_category: Tackling the problem of under-reporting the number of lobbyists
source_quote: a calculation of total full-time equivalents for that organisation (given that some employees may be engaged in interest representation activities for only some of their time) (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:79-81)
notes: FTE calculation captures part-time lobbying that headcount alone misses.

indicator_text: The register secretariat must enforce FTE calculation guidelines uniformly, not allowing different calculations to be used in combination with explanations from registrants.
section_or_category: Tackling the problem of under-reporting the number of lobbyists
source_quote: it is vital that the register secretariat enforce them uniformly (i.e. that they do not allow different calculations to be used, in combination with an explanation from the registrant) (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:82-84)
notes: Comparability requirement; rejects per-registrant methodology.

indicator_text: Enable more comprehensive and effective data checking, including systematic checking of all new entries.
section_or_category: More comprehensive and effective data checking
source_quote: it is vital that more comprehensive and effective data checking is enabled, including systematic checking of all new entries. (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:88-89)
notes: Targets new-entry verification specifically.

indicator_text: Document changes in registrations with a flagging system that enables proper monitoring and allows the public to make comparisons both between organisations and over time.
source_quote: Changes in registrations should be documented with a flagging system, enabling proper monitoring to happen and allowing for the public to make comparisons both between organisations and over time. (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:91-93)
notes: Audit-trail / change-history feature. Generalizes directly.

indicator_text: The European Commission should provide comprehensive information online about all meetings and contacts between Commission officials and lobbyists.
source_quote: the European Commission should provide comprehensive information online about all meetings and contacts between Commission officials and lobbyists. (papers/text/ALTER_EU_2013__transparency_register_review.txt:95-97)
notes: Government-side disclosure (meetings/contacts), complementing registrant-side disclosure. EU-specific institution; principle generalizes to executive-branch meeting logs.
